---
title: "NCSS_model_05_02_2026"
format: html
---


```{r setup}
library(beepr)
source("../code/modeling_functions_05_02_2026.R")
source("../code/pedon_pack_functions_05_02_2026.R")  # for `%||%` and helpers

dir.create("../outputs/models_05_02",  showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
dir.create("../outputs/reports_05_02", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
```

```{r config}
# ============================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================

# Set to TRUE to skip nested CV and only fit final production bundles.
# Useful for fast iteration; set to FALSE for thesis-grade reporting.
FAST_MODE <- FALSE

# Outer/inner fold counts for nested CV
OUTER_V <- 5
INNER_V <- 5
GRID_SIZE <- 12   # hyperparameters per inner-fold tune (latin hypercube for XGB)

# Min-class-size thresholds (raised for nested-CV stability;
# series uses two thresholds — see prep-outcomes chunk).
MIN_N_MAP <- c(
  taxorder        = 15,
  taxsuborder     = 15,
  taxgrtgroup     = 15,
  taxsubgrp_mod1  = 12,   # was 20 — rescues Rhodic, Aeric
  taxpartsize     = 20,
  taxonname_clean = 15    # nested-CV; deployment uses min_n=5
)
# Variants to fit per outcome
MODEL_VARIANTS <- tribble(
  ~engine, ~space,
  "rf",    "plain",
  "xgb",   "plain",
  "rf",    "umap_hdb",
  "xgb",   "umap_hdb"
)

# Outcomes to model (ordered: easy -> hard)
OUTCOMES <- c("taxorder", "taxsuborder", "taxgrtgroup",
              "taxsubgrp_mod1", "taxpartsize", "taxonname_clean")


```

```{r load-data}
# ============================================================
# LOAD DATA
# ============================================================
ncss_pack_files <- list.files("../outputs",
                              pattern = "^pedon_pack_150_.*\\.csv$",
                              full.names = TRUE)
ncss_pack_path <- ncss_pack_files[which.max(file.mtime(ncss_pack_files))]
cat("Loading:", ncss_pack_path, "\n")

pedon_pack_150 <- read.csv(ncss_pack_path) %>%
  mutate(peiid = as.character(peiid))

cat("Pedons loaded:", nrow(pedon_pack_150), "\n")
cat("Columns:",       ncol(pedon_pack_150), "\n")
```

```{r class-coverage-report}
# ============================================================
# CLASS-COVERAGE REPORT
# Runs BEFORE any modeling. Documents what survives each
# threshold and what gets dropped.
# ============================================================

class_coverage_report <- function(df, outcome, min_n) {
  total_pedons  <- sum(!is.na(df[[outcome]]))
  class_counts  <- df %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[outcome]])) %>%
    count(.data[[outcome]], name = "n_pedons", sort = TRUE)

  kept    <- class_counts %>% filter(n_pedons >= min_n)
  dropped <- class_counts %>% filter(n_pedons <  min_n)

  list(
    outcome      = outcome,
    min_n        = min_n,
    total_pedons = total_pedons,
    total_classes = nrow(class_counts),
    kept_classes  = nrow(kept),
    kept_pedons   = sum(kept$n_pedons),
    dropped_classes = nrow(dropped),
    dropped_pedons  = sum(dropped$n_pedons),
    coverage_pct  = round(100 * sum(kept$n_pedons) / total_pedons, 1),
    kept_table    = kept,
    dropped_table = dropped
  )
}

coverage_reports <- map(
  OUTCOMES,
  ~ class_coverage_report(pedon_pack_150, .x, MIN_N_MAP[[.x]])
) %>% set_names(OUTCOMES)

# ---- Summary table (thesis-ready) ----
coverage_summary <- map_dfr(coverage_reports, function(r) {
  tibble(
    outcome           = r$outcome,
    threshold_min_n   = r$min_n,
    total_classes     = r$total_classes,
    kept_classes      = r$kept_classes,
    dropped_classes   = r$dropped_classes,
    total_pedons      = r$total_pedons,
    kept_pedons       = r$kept_pedons,
    dropped_pedons    = r$dropped_pedons,
    coverage_pct      = r$coverage_pct
  )
})

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("CLASS COVERAGE SUMMARY (NCSS)\n")
cat("========================================\n")
print(coverage_summary)

# ---- Per-outcome detail: which classes were dropped? ----
for (oc in OUTCOMES) {
  r <- coverage_reports[[oc]]
  cat("\n--- ", oc, " (min_n = ", r$min_n, ") ---\n", sep = "")
  cat("KEPT (", r$kept_classes, " classes, ", r$kept_pedons, " pedons):\n", sep = "")
  print(r$kept_table)
  if (nrow(r$dropped_table) > 0) {
    cat("\nDROPPED (", r$dropped_classes, " classes, ",
        r$dropped_pedons, " pedons):\n", sep = "")
    print(r$dropped_table)
  } else {
    cat("\nDROPPED: none\n")
  }
}

# ---- Save ----
write.csv(coverage_summary,
          "../outputs/reports_05_02/class_coverage_summary_ncss.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

# Per-outcome dropped classes (long format, for thesis appendix)
dropped_long <- map_dfr(coverage_reports, function(r) {
  if (nrow(r$dropped_table) == 0) return(NULL)
  r$dropped_table %>%
    mutate(outcome = r$outcome, threshold_min_n = r$min_n) %>%
    rename(class = !!sym(r$outcome)) %>%
    select(outcome, threshold_min_n, class, n_pedons)
})

write.csv(dropped_long,
          "../outputs/reports_05_02/dropped_classes_ncss.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nCoverage reports saved to ../outputs/reports_05_02/\n")
```

```{r prep-outcomes}
# ============================================================
# PREP DATA PER OUTCOME
# Filter, factorize, droplevels.
# ============================================================
prepped_data <- map(
  OUTCOMES,
  function(oc) {
    df <- pedon_pack_150 %>%
      select(-any_of(setdiff(TAXONOMY_DROP_COLS, oc))) %>%
      prep_outcome(oc, min_n = MIN_N_MAP[[oc]])

    list(
      outcome = oc,
      df      = df,
      n       = nrow(df),
      n_class = nlevels(df[[oc]])
    )
  }
) %>% set_names(OUTCOMES)

cat("\n--- Final modeling data per outcome ---\n")
walk(prepped_data, ~ cat(.x$outcome, ": n =", .x$n,
                          "| classes =", .x$n_class, "\n"))
```

```{r split}
# ============================================================
# 80/20 train/test split per outcome
# Used for the FINAL production bundles. Nested CV uses its own
# outer folds on the FULL prepped data.
# ============================================================
set.seed(2026)
splits <- map(prepped_data, function(p) {
  s <- initial_split(p$df, prop = 0.8, strata = !!sym(p$outcome))
  list(train = training(s), test = testing(s))
})

cat("\n--- Split sizes ---\n")
walk2(splits, names(splits), ~ cat(.y, ": train =", nrow(.x$train),
                                     "| test =", nrow(.x$test), "\n"))
```

```{r nested-cv}
# ============================================================
# NESTED CV: outer 5 x inner 5 for each {outcome, engine, space}
# This produces honest performance estimates with hyperparameter
# tuning happening inside each outer fold.
# ============================================================

if (!FAST_MODE) {

  cat("\n========================================\n")
  cat("NESTED CV (5 outer x 5 inner)\n")
  cat("This will take a while -- estimated hours.\n")
  cat("========================================\n")

  nested_cv_results <- list()

  for (oc in OUTCOMES) {
    p <- prepped_data[[oc]]
    cat("\n>>> Outcome:", oc, "(", p$n, "pedons,", p$n_class, "classes)\n")

    for (i in seq_len(nrow(MODEL_VARIANTS))) {
      eng <- MODEL_VARIANTS$engine[i]
      sp  <- MODEL_VARIANTS$space[i]
      key <- paste(oc, eng, sp, sep = "_")

      cat("  -- Variant:", eng, sp, "...\n")
      t0 <- Sys.time()

      fold_metrics <- tryCatch(
        nested_cv_one_variant(
          df = p$df %>% select(-any_of(setdiff(TAXONOMY_DROP_COLS, oc))),
          outcome = oc, engine = eng, space = sp,
          outer_v = OUTER_V, inner_v = INNER_V,
          grid_size = GRID_SIZE, seed = 100 + i
        ),
        error = function(e) {
          cat("     ERROR:", e$message, "\n")
          tibble(fold = NA, accuracy = NA_real_, kap = NA_real_,
                 bal_accuracy = NA_real_, f_meas_macro = NA_real_,
                 n_test = NA_integer_, engine = eng, space = sp,
                 outcome = oc)
        }
      )

      dt <- round(as.numeric(difftime(Sys.time(), t0, units = "mins")), 1)
      cat("     done in", dt, "min\n")

      nested_cv_results[[key]] <- fold_metrics
    }
  }

  nested_cv_long <- bind_rows(nested_cv_results)

  # ---- Summary: mean +/- SD across outer folds ----
  nested_cv_summary <- nested_cv_long %>%
    filter(!is.na(fold)) %>%
    group_by(outcome, engine, space) %>%
    summarise(
      n_folds          = n(),
      accuracy_mean    = mean(accuracy,     na.rm = TRUE),
      accuracy_sd      = sd(accuracy,       na.rm = TRUE),
      kap_mean         = mean(kap,          na.rm = TRUE),
      kap_sd           = sd(kap,            na.rm = TRUE),
      bal_acc_mean     = mean(bal_accuracy, na.rm = TRUE),
      bal_acc_sd       = sd(bal_accuracy,   na.rm = TRUE),
      f_meas_mean      = mean(f_meas_macro, na.rm = TRUE),
      f_meas_sd        = sd(f_meas_macro,   na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(outcome, desc(kap_mean))

  cat("\n========================================\n")
  cat("NESTED-CV SUMMARY\n")
  cat("========================================\n")
  print(nested_cv_summary, n = Inf)

  write.csv(nested_cv_long,
            "../outputs/reports_05_02/nested_cv_long_ncss.csv",
            row.names = FALSE)
  write.csv(nested_cv_summary,
            "../outputs/reports_05_02/nested_cv_summary_ncss.csv",
            row.names = FALSE)

  saveRDS(nested_cv_results,
          "../outputs/reports_05_02/nested_cv_results_ncss.rds")

  beep("wilhelm")
} else {
  cat("FAST_MODE = TRUE: skipping nested CV.\n")
  nested_cv_summary <- tibble()
}
```

```{r final-bundles}
# ============================================================
# REPEATED 80/20 SPLITS (Option A): N replicates per bundle
# Each replicate's bundle is saved to disk so Perry can deploy
# all of them and compute deployment-side error bars too.
# ============================================================

N_REPLICATES <- 10  # number of repeated 80/20 splits

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("REPEATED 80/20 SPLITS:", N_REPLICATES, "replicates\n")
cat("Each (outcome × variant) gets fit", N_REPLICATES, "times.\n")
cat("All replicate bundles saved to disk for Perry deployment.\n")
cat("========================================\n")

all_test_metrics_per_rep <- list()

for (rep in seq_len(N_REPLICATES)) {

  cat("\n>>> REPLICATE", rep, "of", N_REPLICATES, "\n")

  # Fresh stratified split per replicate
  set.seed(2026 + rep * 1000)
  splits_rep <- map(prepped_data, function(p) {
    s <- initial_split(p$df, prop = 0.8, strata = !!sym(p$outcome))
    list(train = training(s), test = testing(s))
  })

  metrics_this_rep <- list()

  for (oc in OUTCOMES) {
    s <- splits_rep[[oc]]

    for (i in seq_len(nrow(MODEL_VARIANTS))) {
      eng <- MODEL_VARIANTS$engine[i]
      sp  <- MODEL_VARIANTS$space[i]
      bundle_name <- paste(oc, eng, sp, sep = "_")

      # Naming convention: rep tagged in filename
      rep_bundle_name <- paste0(bundle_name, "_rep", sprintf("%02d", rep))

      cat("  -- rep", rep, "|", bundle_name, "...")
      t0 <- Sys.time()

      res <- tryCatch(
        fit_one_model(
          train_df = s$train %>% select(-any_of(setdiff(TAXONOMY_DROP_COLS, oc))),
          test_df  = s$test  %>% select(-any_of(setdiff(TAXONOMY_DROP_COLS, oc))),
          outcome = oc, engine = eng, space = sp,
          inner_v = INNER_V, grid_size = GRID_SIZE,
          seed = 200 + i + rep * 1000
        ),
        error = function(e) {
          cat(" ERROR:", e$message, "\n")
          NULL
        }
      )

      if (is.null(res)) next

      eval_metrics <- evaluate_predictions(
        res$pred, truth_col = oc,
        k_for_topk = if (oc == "taxonname_clean") 5 else 3
      ) %>%
        mutate(outcome = oc, engine = eng, space = sp,
               bundle_name = bundle_name, replicate = rep)

      metrics_this_rep[[bundle_name]] <- eval_metrics

      # Build bundle (same name as before — replicate ID in $replicate field
      # AND in the filename, but the bundle's $name is the canonical one
      # so Perry's deployment logic doesn't have to change)
      if (sp == "plain") {
        bundle <- build_plain_bundle(
          name = bundle_name, outcome = oc,
          train_raw = res$train_raw,
          prep_rec  = res$prep_rec,
          final_fit = res$final_fit
        )
      } else {
        # UMAP file goes alongside the bundle, also tagged with rep
        umap_path <- paste0("../outputs/models_05_02/", rep_bundle_name, "_umap.uwot")
        uwot::save_uwot(res$umap_fit, file = umap_path)

        bundle <- build_umap_hdb_bundle(
          name = bundle_name, outcome = oc,
          train_raw = res$train_raw,
          prep_rec  = res$prep_rec,
          final_fit = res$final_fit,
          umap_model_file = umap_path,
          hdb_fit = res$hdb_fit, z_train = res$z_train,
          umap_settings = list(n_components = 10, n_neighbors = 15,
                               min_dist = 0.1, metric = "euclidean")
        )
      }

      bundle$test_metrics <- eval_metrics
      bundle$replicate <- rep
      bundle$conf_mat <- conf_mat(
        res$pred %>% rename(.truth = all_of(oc)),
        truth = .truth, estimate = .pred_class
      )

      # Save with rep-tagged filename
      saveRDS(bundle, paste0("../outputs/models_05_02/", rep_bundle_name, "_bundle.rds"))

      dt <- round(as.numeric(difftime(Sys.time(), t0, units = "mins")), 1)
      cat(" done in", dt, "min | kap =", round(eval_metrics$kap, 3), "\n")
    }
  }

  all_test_metrics_per_rep[[rep]] <- bind_rows(metrics_this_rep)
  beep("ping")
}

# Combine all metrics across replicates
test_metrics_all_reps <- bind_rows(all_test_metrics_per_rep)

write.csv(test_metrics_all_reps,
          "../outputs/reports_05_02/test_metrics_all_reps_ncss.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nFinished all", N_REPLICATES, "replicates.\n")
beep("wilhelm")
```

```{r aggregate-metrics}
# ============================================================
# AGGREGATE METRICS + PICK CANONICAL BUNDLE PER (outcome × variant)
# Canonical = replicate whose kap is closest to the median.
# That bundle gets saved without the _repNN suffix so Perry's
# existing logic finds it by default.
# ============================================================

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("PICKING CANONICAL BUNDLES (median by kap)\n")
cat("========================================\n")

# Summary stats per (outcome × variant) across replicates
test_metrics_summary <- test_metrics_all_reps %>%
  group_by(outcome, engine, space, bundle_name) %>%
  summarise(
    n_replicates    = n(),
    accuracy_mean   = mean(accuracy,     na.rm = TRUE),
    accuracy_sd     = sd(accuracy,       na.rm = TRUE),
    accuracy_median = median(accuracy,   na.rm = TRUE),
    accuracy_lo_95  = quantile(accuracy, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    accuracy_hi_95  = quantile(accuracy, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
    kap_mean        = mean(kap,          na.rm = TRUE),
    kap_sd          = sd(kap,            na.rm = TRUE),
    kap_median      = median(kap,        na.rm = TRUE),
    kap_lo_95       = quantile(kap, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    kap_hi_95       = quantile(kap, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
    bal_acc_mean    = mean(bal_accuracy, na.rm = TRUE),
    bal_acc_sd      = sd(bal_accuracy,   na.rm = TRUE),
    bal_acc_median  = median(bal_accuracy, na.rm = TRUE),
    f_meas_mean     = mean(f_meas_macro, na.rm = TRUE),
    f_meas_sd       = sd(f_meas_macro,   na.rm = TRUE),
    top_k_mean      = mean(top_k,        na.rm = TRUE),
    top_k_sd        = sd(top_k,          na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(test_metrics_summary, n = Inf)

write.csv(test_metrics_summary,
          "../outputs/reports_05_02/test_metrics_summary_repeated_ncss.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

# For each (outcome × variant), find replicate closest to median kap
canonical_picks <- test_metrics_all_reps %>%
  left_join(
    test_metrics_summary %>% select(outcome, engine, space, kap_median),
    by = c("outcome", "engine", "space")
  ) %>%
  mutate(dist_to_median = abs(kap - kap_median)) %>%
  group_by(outcome, engine, space, bundle_name) %>%
  slice_min(dist_to_median, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(outcome, engine, space, bundle_name, replicate,
         kap, kap_median, dist_to_median)

cat("\n--- Canonical replicate per bundle (closest to median kap) ---\n")
print(canonical_picks)

write.csv(canonical_picks,
          "../outputs/reports_05_02/canonical_replicate_picks.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

# Copy the canonical replicate's bundle to its canonical filename
cat("\nCopying canonical bundles...\n")
for (i in seq_len(nrow(canonical_picks))) {
  pick <- canonical_picks[i, ]
  rep <- pick$replicate
  bn  <- pick$bundle_name

  src_bundle <- paste0("../outputs/models_05_02/", bn,
                        "_rep", sprintf("%02d", rep), "_bundle.rds")
  dst_bundle <- paste0("../outputs/models_05_02/", bn, "_bundle.rds")

  if (!file.exists(src_bundle)) {
    cat("  WARNING: missing", src_bundle, "\n")
    next
  }

  # Load, attach summary stats, save with canonical name
  b <- readRDS(src_bundle)
  b$repeated_split_summary <- test_metrics_summary %>%
    filter(bundle_name == bn) %>%
    as.list()
  saveRDS(b, dst_bundle)

  # Copy associated UMAP file too
  if ((b$model_type %||% "") == "umap_hdb") {
    src_umap <- paste0("../outputs/models_05_02/", bn,
                        "_rep", sprintf("%02d", rep), "_umap.uwot")
    dst_umap <- paste0("../outputs/models_05_02/", bn, "_umap.uwot")
    if (file.exists(src_umap)) {
      file.copy(src_umap, dst_umap, overwrite = TRUE)
    }
  }

  cat("  ", bn, "(rep", rep, ", kap =", round(pick$kap, 3), ")\n")
}

cat("\nCanonical bundles saved. Replicate bundles preserved with _repNN suffix.\n")
beep("complete")
```


```{r series-final-bundle-low-threshold}
# ============================================================
# OPTIONAL: refit series with min_n=5 for higher coverage,
# stored as a separate set of bundles.
# Use these for actual deployment if you want broader series
# coverage; nested-CV results above are at min_n=15.
# ============================================================

if ("taxonname_clean" %in% OUTCOMES) {

  cat("\n>>> Refitting series at min_n=5 for deployment coverage\n")

  df_series_lowthresh <- pedon_pack_150 %>%
    select(-any_of(setdiff(TAXONOMY_DROP_COLS, "taxonname_clean"))) %>%
    prep_outcome("taxonname_clean", min_n = 5) # nested CV used min_n=15, but we can fit more classes for deployment since we won't be doing nested CV there. This is a bit hacky but it lets us reuse the same code and compare results easily. (this is useful for the Greenville series n = 8, which is important to include in deployment but too small for nested CV).

  cat("   n =", nrow(df_series_lowthresh),
      "| classes =", nlevels(df_series_lowthresh$taxonname_clean), "\n")

  set.seed(2027)
  s5 <- initial_split(df_series_lowthresh, prop = 0.8, strata = taxonname_clean)
  train_5 <- training(s5); test_5 <- testing(s5)

  for (i in seq_len(nrow(MODEL_VARIANTS))) {
    eng <- MODEL_VARIANTS$engine[i]
    sp  <- MODEL_VARIANTS$space[i]
    bn  <- paste("taxonname_clean_min5", eng, sp, sep = "_")

    cat("  -- Fitting:", bn, "\n")
    res <- tryCatch(
      fit_one_model(
        train_df = train_5, test_df = test_5,
        outcome = "taxonname_clean", engine = eng, space = sp,
        inner_v = 3,  # smaller because per-class N is small
        grid_size = 10, seed = 300 + i
      ),
      error = function(e) { cat("     ERROR:", e$message, "\n"); NULL }
    )
    if (is.null(res)) next

    eval_metrics <- evaluate_predictions(
      res$pred, truth_col = "taxonname_clean", k_for_topk = 5
    ) %>% mutate(bundle_name = bn)

    if (sp == "plain") {
      bundle <- build_plain_bundle(
        name = bn, outcome = "taxonname_clean",
        train_raw = res$train_raw,
        prep_rec  = res$prep_rec,
        final_fit = res$final_fit
      )
    } else {
      umap_path <- paste0("../outputs/models_05_02/", bn, "_umap.uwot")
      uwot::save_uwot(res$umap_fit, file = umap_path)
      bundle <- build_umap_hdb_bundle(
        name = bn, outcome = "taxonname_clean",
        train_raw = res$train_raw, prep_rec = res$prep_rec,
        final_fit = res$final_fit, umap_model_file = umap_path,
        hdb_fit = res$hdb_fit, z_train = res$z_train,
        umap_settings = list(n_components = 10, n_neighbors = 15,
                             min_dist = 0.1, metric = "euclidean")
      )
    }
    bundle$test_metrics <- eval_metrics
    bundle$threshold_note <- "min_n=5 (deployment); nested CV used min_n=15"

    saveRDS(bundle, paste0("../outputs/models_05_02/", bn, "_bundle.rds"))
    cat("     kap =", round(eval_metrics$kap, 3),
        "| top-5 =", round(eval_metrics$top_k, 3), "\n")
  }
}
```

```{r combined-summary}
# ============================================================
# COMBINED RESULTS TABLE: nested CV + test metrics + best per outcome
# ============================================================

if (!FAST_MODE && nrow(nested_cv_summary) > 0) {

  combined <- nested_cv_summary %>%
    left_join(
      test_metrics_long %>%
        select(outcome, engine, space,
               test_accuracy = accuracy,
               test_kap = kap,
               test_bal_acc = bal_accuracy,
               test_f1 = f_meas_macro,
               test_topk = top_k),
      by = c("outcome", "engine", "space")
    )

  cat("\n========================================\n")
  cat("COMBINED RESULTS: NESTED CV + TEST SET\n")
  cat("========================================\n")
  print(combined, n = Inf)

  write.csv(combined,
            "../outputs/reports_05_02/combined_results_ncss.csv",
            row.names = FALSE)

  # ---- Best per outcome by each metric ----
  cat("\n--- Best variant per outcome by KAP ---\n")
  combined %>%
    group_by(outcome) %>%
    slice_max(kap_mean, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    select(outcome, engine, space, kap_mean, kap_sd,
           bal_acc_mean, f_meas_mean, test_kap) %>%
    print()

  cat("\n--- Best variant per outcome by BAL_ACCURACY ---\n")
  combined %>%
    group_by(outcome) %>%
    slice_max(bal_acc_mean, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    select(outcome, engine, space, bal_acc_mean, bal_acc_sd,
           kap_mean, f_meas_mean, test_bal_acc) %>%
    print()

  cat("\n--- Best variant per outcome by MACRO F1 ---\n")
  combined %>%
    group_by(outcome) %>%
    slice_max(f_meas_mean, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    select(outcome, engine, space, f_meas_mean, f_meas_sd,
           kap_mean, bal_acc_mean, test_f1) %>%
    print()
}

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("DONE.\n")
cat("- Class coverage: ../outputs/reports_05_02/class_coverage_summary_ncss.csv\n")
cat("- Dropped classes: ../outputs/reports_05_02/dropped_classes_ncss.csv\n")
cat("- Nested CV (long): ../outputs/reports_05_02/nested_cv_long_ncss.csv\n")
cat("- Nested CV (summary): ../outputs/reports_05_02/nested_cv_summary_ncss.csv\n")
cat("- Test metrics: ../outputs/reports_05_02/test_metrics_ncss.csv\n")
cat("- Combined results: ../outputs/reports_05_02/combined_results_ncss.csv\n")
cat("- Bundles: ../outputs/models_05_02/*_bundle.rds\n")
cat("========================================\n")
beep("complete")
```

```{r figures!}

library(ggplot2)

tax_order <- c("taxorder", "taxsuborder", "taxgrtgroup",
               "taxsubgrp_mod1", "taxpartsize", "taxonname_clean")
tax_labels <- c("Order", "Suborder", "Great Group",
                "Subgroup", "Particle Size", "Series")
names(tax_labels) <- tax_order

ncss_model_compare <- nested_cv_summary %>%
  mutate(
    outcome = factor(outcome, levels = tax_order, labels = tax_labels[tax_order]),
    variant = paste(engine, space, sep = " / "),
    variant = recode(variant,
                     "rf / plain" = "RF (plain)",
                     "xgb / plain" = "XGB (plain)",
                     "rf / umap_hdb" = "RF (UMAP+HDB)",
                     "xgb / umap_hdb" = "XGB (UMAP+HDB)")
  )

fig_ncss <- ggplot(ncss_model_compare,
                   aes(x = outcome, y = kap_mean, color = engine, shape = space)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = kap_mean - kap_sd, ymax = kap_mean + kap_sd),
                width = 0.25, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_point(size = 3.5, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("rf" = "#2C7BB6", "xgb" = "#D7191C"),
                     labels = c("Random Forest", "XGBoost"),
                     name = "Engine") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("plain" = 16, "umap_hdb" = 17),
                     labels = c("Plain features", "UMAP + HDBSCAN"),
                     name = "Feature space") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Cohen's Kappa (mean ± SD across outer folds)",
       title = "NCSS nested cross-validation: model variant comparison",
       subtitle = "Plain features outperform UMAP+HDBSCAN at every taxonomic level") +
  thesis_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

print(fig_ncss)
ggsave("../outputs/figures_05_02/fig_ncss_model_compare.png",
       fig_ncss, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

fig_ncss_heat <- ggplot(ncss_model_compare,
                        aes(x = outcome, y = variant, fill = kap_mean)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", kap_mean)), size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#FDAE61", high = "#1A9641",
                      limits = c(0.50, 0.90), name = "Kappa") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL,
       title = "NCSS nested CV: model variants × taxonomic levels") +
  thesis_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))
print(fig_ncss_heat)
ggsave("../outputs/figures_05_02/fig_ncss_heatmap.png",
       fig_ncss_heat, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(here)

# Load both files
nested_cv <- read.csv(here("outputs", "reports_05_02", "nested_cv_summary_ncss.csv"))
ncss_pack_path <- list.files(here("outputs"),
                              pattern = "^pedon_pack_150_.*\\.csv$",
                              full.names = TRUE)
ncss_pack_path <- ncss_pack_path[which.max(file.mtime(ncss_pack_path))]
ncss <- read.csv(ncss_pack_path)

# Per-outcome thresholds (what you actually deployed)
threshold_map <- tibble::tribble(
  ~outcome,           ~min_n,
  "taxorder",         15,
  "taxsuborder",      15,
  "taxgrtgroup",      15,
  "taxsubgrp_mod1",   12,
  "taxpartsize",      20,
  "taxonname_clean",  5    # using min_n=5 as the primary series threshold
)

# Compute class and pedon retention for each outcome
coverage <- ncss %>%
  pivot_longer(c(taxorder, taxsuborder, taxgrtgroup, taxsubgrp_mod1,
                 taxpartsize, taxonname_clean),
               names_to = "outcome", values_to = "class") %>%
  filter(!is.na(class), class != "") %>%
  count(outcome, class) %>%
  left_join(threshold_map, by = "outcome") %>%
  mutate(retained = n >= min_n) %>%
  group_by(outcome) %>%
  summarise(
    n_classes_total      = n(),
    n_classes_retained   = sum(retained),
    n_classes_dropped    = sum(!retained),
    n_pedons_total       = sum(n),
    n_pedons_retained    = sum(n[retained]),
    n_pedons_dropped     = sum(n[!retained]),
    pct_classes_retained = round(100 * n_classes_retained / n_classes_total, 1),
    pct_pedons_retained  = round(100 * n_pedons_retained / n_pedons_total, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(coverage)

# Pivot to long for plotting
coverage_long <- coverage %>%
  select(outcome,
         `Classes` = pct_classes_retained,
         `Pedons` = pct_pedons_retained,
         n_classes_dropped, n_classes_retained,
         n_pedons_dropped, n_pedons_retained) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Classes, Pedons),
               names_to = "level", values_to = "pct_retained") %>%
  mutate(
    pct_dropped = 100 - pct_retained,
    n_dropped = if_else(level == "Classes", n_classes_dropped, n_pedons_dropped),
    n_retained = if_else(level == "Classes", n_classes_retained, n_pedons_retained)
  )

# Order outcomes coarse to fine
tax_order  <- c("taxorder", "taxsuborder", "taxgrtgroup",
                "taxsubgrp_mod1", "taxpartsize", "taxonname_clean")
tax_labels <- c("Order", "Suborder", "Great Group",
                "Subgroup", "Particle Size", "Series")
names(tax_labels) <- tax_order

coverage_long <- coverage_long %>%
  mutate(outcome = factor(outcome,
                          levels = tax_order,
                          labels = tax_labels[tax_order]),
         level = factor(level, levels = c("Pedons", "Classes")))  # Pedons on top reads better

# Build the plot
fig_coverage <- ggplot(coverage_long,
                       aes(x = outcome, y = pct_retained, fill = level)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(pct_retained, "%")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
            vjust = -0.4, size = 3.4, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0("(", n_retained, "/", n_retained + n_dropped, ")")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
            vjust = 1.5, size = 3, color = "white") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Pedons" = "#2C7BB6",
                                "Classes" = "#D7191C"),
                    name = NULL,
                    labels = c("% pedons retained", "% classes retained")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 110),
                     breaks = seq(0, 100, 25),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Retained (%)",
       title = "Class and pedon retention after minimum-sample filtering",
       subtitle = "Most pedons are retained even when most rare classes are dropped") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "top",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1))

print(fig_coverage)

# Save
ggsave(here("outputs", "figures_05_02", "ncss_class_coverage_pct.png"),
       fig_coverage, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
# Also save the underlying numbers as a CSV for the thesis
write.csv(coverage,
          here("outputs", "figures_05_02", "ncss_class_coverage_table.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)


# Pedon-weighted retention
dropped_summary <- dropped %>%
  left_join(min_n_map, by = "outcome") %>%
  filter(n_pedons < min_n) %>%
  group_by(outcome) %>%
  summarise(
    n_classes_dropped = n(),
    n_pedons_dropped = sum(n_pedons),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(dropped_summary)

# Apply min_n = 5 threshold for series only
series_min5_summary <- ncss %>%
  filter(!is.na(taxonname_clean)) %>%
  count(taxonname_clean) %>%
  mutate(retained = n >= 5) %>%
  summarise(
    n_classes_retained = sum(retained),
    n_classes_dropped  = sum(!retained),
    n_pedons_retained  = sum(n[retained]),
    n_pedons_dropped   = sum(n[!retained]),
    pct_pedons_retained = round(100 * n_pedons_retained /
                                  (n_pedons_retained + n_pedons_dropped), 1)
  )
print(series_min5_summary)
library(tidyverse)
library(here)

reps <- read.csv(here("outputs", "reports_05_02",
                      "test_metrics_all_reps_ncss.csv"))

summary_table <- reps %>%
  group_by(outcome, engine, space) %>%
  summarise(
    n_reps          = n(),
    kap_median      = median(kap),
    kap_lo_95       = quantile(kap, 0.025),
    kap_hi_95       = quantile(kap, 0.975),
    kap_mean        = mean(kap),
    kap_sd          = sd(kap),
    accuracy_median = median(accuracy),
    accuracy_lo_95  = quantile(accuracy, 0.025),
    accuracy_hi_95  = quantile(accuracy, 0.975),
    bal_acc_median  = median(bal_accuracy, na.rm = TRUE),
    bal_acc_lo_95   = quantile(bal_accuracy, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    bal_acc_hi_95   = quantile(bal_accuracy, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
    f_meas_median   = median(f_meas_macro, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(outcome, desc(kap_median))

print(summary_table, n = Inf)

write.csv(summary_table,
          here("outputs", "reports_05_02", "test_metrics_summary_clean.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)
# Pull NCSS summary from the repeated splits
  ncss_for_plot <- read.csv("../outputs/reports_05_02/test_metrics_summary_clean.csv") %>%
  group_by(outcome) %>%
  slice_max(kap_median, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    dataset = "NCSS (internal test)",
    kap_value = kap_median,
    kap_lo = kap_lo_95,
    kap_hi = kap_hi_95
  )

# Perry: point estimate, no CI
perry_for_plot <- perry_metric_summary %>%
  filter(!is_min5 | outcome != "taxonname_clean") %>%
  group_by(outcome) %>%
  slice_max(kap, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    dataset = "Perry (external)",
    kap_value = kap,
    kap_lo = NA_real_,    # no error bars on Perry
    kap_hi = NA_real_
  )

fig1_data <- bind_rows(
  ncss_for_plot %>% select(outcome, engine, space, dataset,
                            kap_value, kap_lo, kap_hi),
  perry_for_plot %>% select(outcome, engine, space, dataset,
                             kap_value, kap_lo, kap_hi)
) %>%
  left_join(n_classes_lookup, by = "outcome") %>%
  mutate(
    outcome = factor(outcome, levels = tax_order, labels = tax_labels[tax_order]),
    dataset = factor(dataset, levels = c("NCSS (internal test)", "Perry (external)"))
  )

fig1 <- ggplot(fig1_data, aes(x = outcome, y = kap_value, fill = dataset)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = kap_lo, ymax = kap_hi),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
                width = 0.25, na.rm = TRUE, linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", kap_value)),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
            vjust = -0.4, size = 3.2) +
  geom_text(data = fig1_data %>% distinct(outcome, n_classes_max),
            aes(x = outcome, y = 1.06, label = paste0("k=", n_classes_max)),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 3.2, fontface = "italic", color = "grey30") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("NCSS (internal test)" = "#2C7BB6",
                                "Perry (external)" = "#D7191C"),
                    name = NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.10), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Cohen's Kappa",
       title = "Model performance degrades from coarse to fine taxonomic levels\n",
       subtitle = "NCSS error bars: 95% range across 10 train/test replicates;\n Perry: point estimate from canonical bundle") +
  thesis_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))
print(fig1)
reps <- read.csv(here("outputs", "reports_05_02", "test_metrics_all_reps_ncss.csv"))

reps_for_plot <- reps %>%
  mutate(
    outcome = factor(outcome, levels = tax_order, labels = tax_labels[tax_order]),
    variant = paste(engine, space, sep = " / "),
    variant = recode(variant,
                     "rf / plain"     = "RF (plain)",
                     "xgb / plain"    = "XGB (plain)",
                     "rf / umap_hdb"  = "RF (UMAP+HDB)",
                     "xgb / umap_hdb" = "XGB (UMAP+HDB)")
  )

fig_variability <- ggplot(reps_for_plot,
                          aes(x = outcome, y = kap, fill = variant)) +
  geom_boxplot(position = position_dodge(width = 0.85),
               width = 0.7, outlier.size = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("RF (plain)" = "#2C7BB6",
                                "XGB (plain)" = "#1A9641",
                                "RF (UMAP+HDB)" = "#FDAE61",
                                "XGB (UMAP+HDB)" = "#D7191C"),
                    name = NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.2, 1.05), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Cohen's Kappa",
       title = "NCSS internal performance variability across 10 train/test splits\n",
       subtitle = "Plain features outperform UMAP+HDB at every taxonomic level,\n with tighter distributions") +
  thesis_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "top")

print(fig_variability)
ggsave(here("outputs", "figures_05_02", "fig_variability_boxplots.png"),
       fig_variability, width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(yardstick)
library(patchwork)

fig_dir <- here("outputs", "figures_05_02")
dir.create(fig_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

thesis_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "top",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA))

tax_order  <- c("taxorder", "taxsuborder", "taxgrtgroup",
                "taxsubgrp_mod1", "taxpartsize", "taxonname_clean")
tax_labels <- c("Order", "Suborder", "Great Group",
                "Subgroup", "Particle Size", "Series")
names(tax_labels) <- tax_order

# Load all canonical bundles (skip _repNN_)
bundle_files <- list.files(here("outputs", "models_05_02"),
                           pattern = "_bundle\\.rds$",
                           full.names = TRUE)
bundle_files <- bundle_files[
  !str_detect(basename(bundle_files), "_rep[0-9]+_bundle\\.rds$") &
    basename(bundle_files) != "taxsubgrp_xgb_plain_bundle.rds"
]
bundles <- map(bundle_files, readRDS) %>%
  set_names(map_chr(., "name"))

# Pick the best variant per outcome (by kap_median from repeated splits)
summary_clean <- read.csv(here("outputs", "reports_05_02",
                                "test_metrics_summary_clean.csv"))
best_per_outcome <- summary_clean %>%
  group_by(outcome) %>%
  slice_max(kap_median, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(bundle_name = paste(outcome, engine, space, sep = "_"))

cat("Best variant per outcome:\n")
print(best_per_outcome %>% select(outcome, engine, space, kap_median))


plot_ncss_confusion <- function(bundle, max_classes = 12) {
  cm_df <- bundle$conf_mat$table %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    rename(truth = Truth, pred = Prediction, n = Freq)

  # Compute row-proportions (what fraction of each true class went where)
  cm_df <- cm_df %>%
    group_by(truth) %>%
    mutate(
      row_total = sum(n),
      prop = ifelse(row_total == 0, 0, n / row_total)
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    select(-row_total)

  # If too many classes, keep top N by truth frequency
  if (length(unique(cm_df$truth)) > max_classes) {
    keep <- cm_df %>%
      group_by(truth) %>% summarise(total = sum(n), .groups = "drop") %>%
      slice_max(total, n = max_classes) %>% pull(truth) %>% as.character()
    cm_df <- cm_df %>%
      filter(as.character(truth) %in% keep,
             as.character(pred) %in% keep)
  }

  ggplot(cm_df, aes(x = pred, y = truth, fill = prop)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white") +
    geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(n == 0, "", n)), size = 3) +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "#2C7BB6",
                        limits = c(0, 1), name = "Row prop") +
    labs(x = "Predicted", y = "Observed",
         title = tax_labels[bundle$outcome]) +
    thesis_theme +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
          panel.grid = element_blank(),
          legend.position = "right")
}

# Build one CM per outcome using the best variant
ncss_cm_plots <- list()
for (i in seq_len(nrow(best_per_outcome))) {
  oc <- best_per_outcome$outcome[i]
  bn <- best_per_outcome$bundle_name[i]
  b  <- bundles[[bn]]
  if (is.null(b) || is.null(b$conf_mat)) {
    cat("WARNING: missing bundle or conf_mat for", bn, "\n")
    next
  }
  ncss_cm_plots[[oc]] <- plot_ncss_confusion(b, max_classes = 12)
}

# Stack into 3x2 grid
fig_ncss_cm <- (ncss_cm_plots$taxorder      + ncss_cm_plots$taxsuborder) /
               (ncss_cm_plots$taxgrtgroup   + ncss_cm_plots$taxsubgrp_mod1) /
               (ncss_cm_plots$taxpartsize   + ncss_cm_plots$taxonname_clean) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "NCSS internal-test confusion matrices (canonical replicate)",
    subtitle = "Cell color = row-proportion; cell number = pedon count",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))
  )

print(fig_ncss_cm)
ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, "fig_ncss_confusion_matrices.png"),
       fig_ncss_cm, width = 14, height = 15, dpi = 300)

library(vip)

extract_vip <- function(bundle, top_n = 20) {
  # Pull the underlying model object from the workflow
  fit_obj <- extract_fit_engine(bundle$final_fit)
  
  # Get feature names from the recipe
  rec <- bundle$prep_rec
  feature_names <- names(bake(rec, new_data = NULL))
  feature_names <- setdiff(feature_names, c(bundle$outcome, "peiid"))
  
  # Engine-specific importance
  imp <- tryCatch({
    if (inherits(fit_obj, "ranger")) {
      # Random Forest
      tibble(
        feature = names(fit_obj$variable.importance),
        importance = fit_obj$variable.importance
      )
    } else if (inherits(fit_obj, "xgb.Booster")) {
      # XGBoost
      xgb_imp <- xgboost::xgb.importance(model = fit_obj)
      tibble(
        feature = xgb_imp$Feature,
        importance = xgb_imp$Gain
      )
    } else {
      cat("Unknown model class for", bundle$name, "\n")
      return(NULL)
    }
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("VIP error for", bundle$name, ":", e$message, "\n")
    NULL
  })
  
  if (is.null(imp)) return(NULL)
  
  imp %>%
    arrange(desc(importance)) %>%
    slice_head(n = top_n) %>%
    mutate(outcome = bundle$outcome,
           bundle_name = bundle$name)
}

# Extract VIP for each outcome's best variant
vip_data <- map_dfr(seq_len(nrow(best_per_outcome)), function(i) {
  bn <- best_per_outcome$bundle_name[i]
  if (is.null(bundles[[bn]])) return(NULL)
  extract_vip(bundles[[bn]], top_n = 15)
})

# Plot one VIP per outcome (small multiples)
plot_vip <- function(d, title) {
  d %>%
    mutate(feature = fct_reorder(feature, importance)) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = importance, y = feature)) +
    geom_col(fill = "#2C7BB6") +
    labs(x = "Importance", y = NULL, title = title) +
    thesis_theme +
    theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9))
}

vip_plots <- vip_data %>%
  group_split(outcome) %>%
  map(~ plot_vip(.x, tax_labels[unique(.x$outcome)]))

names(vip_plots) <- vip_data %>% distinct(outcome) %>% pull(outcome)

# Stack into 3x2 grid
fig_vip <- (vip_plots$taxorder      + vip_plots$taxsuborder) /
           (vip_plots$taxgrtgroup   + vip_plots$taxsubgrp_mod1) /
           (vip_plots$taxpartsize   + vip_plots$taxonname_clean) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Top 15 predictors per taxonomic level (NCSS-trained, best variant)",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))
  )

print(fig_vip)
ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, "fig_ncss_vip.png"),
       fig_vip, width = 14, height = 15, dpi = 300)

# Also save the underlying VIP table for thesis appendix
write.csv(vip_data,
          here("outputs", "reports_05_02", "ncss_vip_top15.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)


# this is only for training data, so it's not a true "top-k accuracy" that you'd get on unseen data, but it gives a sense of how well the model ranks the true class among its predictions. It's also useful for understanding how many classes the model is effectively distinguishing between, especially at finer taxonomic levels where there are many classes.
top_k_from_bundle <- function(bundle, k_max = 10) {
  oc <- bundle$outcome
  train <- bundle$train_raw
  
  # Predict probabilities on training data
  preds <- predict(bundle$final_fit, new_data = train, type = "prob")
  
  # Get class names
  prob_cols <- grep("^\\.pred_", names(preds), value = TRUE)
  class_names <- str_remove(prob_cols, "^\\.pred_")
  truth <- as.character(train[[oc]])
  
  # For each row, get the rank of the truth class
  rank_of_truth <- map_int(seq_len(nrow(preds)), function(i) {
    p <- as.numeric(preds[i, prob_cols])
    names(p) <- class_names
    sorted <- sort(p, decreasing = TRUE)
    rank <- which(names(sorted) == truth[i])
    if (length(rank) == 0) NA_integer_ else rank
  })
  
  # Top-k accuracy = proportion of pedons where truth is rank <= k
  k_max_actual <- min(k_max, length(class_names))
  tibble(
    outcome = oc,
    k = 1:k_max_actual,
    top_k_accuracy = map_dbl(1:k_max_actual, ~ mean(rank_of_truth <= .x, na.rm = TRUE))
  )
}

# Compute for canonical bundles only (best variant per outcome)
top_k_curves <- map_dfr(seq_len(nrow(best_per_outcome)), function(i) {
  bn <- best_per_outcome$bundle_name[i]
  if (is.null(bundles[[bn]])) return(NULL)
  top_k_from_bundle(bundles[[bn]], k_max = 10)
})

top_k_curves <- top_k_curves %>%
  mutate(outcome = factor(outcome, levels = tax_order, labels = tax_labels[tax_order]))

fig_top_k <- ggplot(top_k_curves,
                    aes(x = k, y = top_k_accuracy, color = outcome)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2", name = NULL) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:10) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), labels = scales::percent_format(),
                     expand = c(0, 0.01)) +
  labs(x = "k (top-k predictions considered)",
       y = "Top-k accuracy",
       title = "Top-k accuracy curves on NCSS training data",
       subtitle = "Best variant per outcome; truth is recovered within top-k for high k") +
  thesis_theme

print(fig_top_k)
ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, "fig_ncss_top_k.png"),
       fig_top_k, width = 9, height = 6, dpi = 300)

#heatmap of top-k accuracy at k=5 for best variant per outcome (TRAINING DATA!!!!)
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(patchwork)

# We need test-set predictions per replicate. Each rep bundle contains
# train_raw but NOT a held-out test set with predictions. We need to
# reconstruct: for each rep, recreate the 80/20 split and predict on test.

# Load all rep-tagged bundles
rep_bundle_files <- list.files(here("outputs", "models_05_02"),
                                pattern = "_rep[0-9]+_bundle\\.rds$",
                                full.names = TRUE)

cat("Found", length(rep_bundle_files), "replicate bundles\n")

# Load the original full NCSS pack to recreate splits
ncss_pack_files <- list.files(here("outputs"),
                              pattern = "^pedon_pack_150_.*\\.csv$",
                              full.names = TRUE)
ncss_pack_path <- ncss_pack_files[which.max(file.mtime(ncss_pack_files))]
ncss_full <- read.csv(ncss_pack_path) %>%
  mutate(peiid = as.character(peiid))

# Helper: get test set for a given replicate using the same seed logic
# as in your NCSS_models.qmd (set.seed(2026 + rep * 1000))
get_test_predictions <- function(bundle, full_data, rep_num) {
  oc <- bundle$outcome
  
  # Recreate the prep_outcome filter (drop classes below min_n)
  source(here("code", "modeling_functions_05_02_2026.R"))
  
  min_n_lookup <- c(taxorder = 15, taxsuborder = 15, taxgrtgroup = 15,
                    taxsubgrp_mod1 = 12, taxpartsize = 20,
                    taxonname_clean = 5)  # using min_n=15 for series to match the main NCSS run
  
  df_prepped <- full_data %>%
    select(-any_of(setdiff(TAXONOMY_DROP_COLS, oc))) %>%
    prep_outcome(oc, min_n = min_n_lookup[[oc]])
  
  # Recreate the split with the same seed
  set.seed(2026 + rep_num * 1000)
  s <- initial_split(df_prepped, prop = 0.8, strata = !!sym(oc))
  test_df <- testing(s)
  
  # Predict probabilities on test set
  preds <- predict(bundle$final_fit, new_data = test_df, type = "prob")
  preds$truth <- as.character(test_df[[oc]])
  preds$peiid <- as.character(test_df$peiid)
  preds$replicate <- rep_num
  preds$outcome <- oc
  preds$bundle_name <- bundle$name
  
  preds
}

# Pick the best variant per outcome (any engine, any space) by median kappa
best_per_outcome <- summary_clean %>%
  group_by(outcome) %>%
  slice_max(kap_median, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(bundle_root = paste(outcome, engine, space, sep = "_"))

cat("Best variant per outcome:\n")
print(best_per_outcome %>% select(outcome, engine, space, kap_median, bundle_root))

# Build rep_meta from all replicate bundles, then filter to the best per outcome
rep_meta <- tibble(
  filepath = rep_bundle_files,
  basename = basename(rep_bundle_files),
  bundle_root = str_remove(basename(rep_bundle_files),
                            "_rep[0-9]+_bundle\\.rds$"),
  rep_num = as.integer(str_extract(basename(rep_bundle_files), "(?<=_rep)[0-9]+"))
) %>%
  filter(bundle_root %in% best_per_outcome$bundle_root)

cat("Processing", nrow(rep_meta), "replicate bundles for rank-of-truth\n")

all_test_preds <- map_dfr(seq_len(nrow(rep_meta)), function(i) {
  rm <- rep_meta[i, ]
  cat("  rep", rm$rep_num, "|", rm$bundle_root, "\n")
  
  b <- readRDS(rm$filepath)
  tryCatch(
    get_test_predictions(b, ncss_full, rm$rep_num),
    error = function(e) {
      cat("    ERROR:", e$message, "\n")
      NULL
    }
  )
})

cat("\nTotal test predictions across all reps:", nrow(all_test_preds), "\n")

compute_rank_and_pred_what <- function(preds_df) {
  prob_cols <- grep("^\\.pred_", names(preds_df), value = TRUE)
  class_names <- str_remove(prob_cols, "^\\.pred_")
  
  # For each pedon, compute ranks for ALL classes
  result <- map_dfr(seq_len(nrow(preds_df)), function(i) {
    p <- as.numeric(preds_df[i, prob_cols])
    names(p) <- class_names
    sorted <- sort(p, decreasing = TRUE)
    
    truth <- preds_df$truth[i]
    if (is.na(truth)) return(NULL)
    
    rank_truth <- which(names(sorted) == truth)
    if (length(rank_truth) == 0) rank_truth <- NA_integer_
    
    tibble(
      peiid = preds_df$peiid[i],
      replicate = preds_df$replicate[i],
      outcome = preds_df$outcome[i],
      truth = truth,
      rank1_class = names(sorted)[1],
      rank2_class = if (length(sorted) >= 2) names(sorted)[2] else NA_character_,
      rank3_class = if (length(sorted) >= 3) names(sorted)[3] else NA_character_,
      rank_of_truth = rank_truth,
      pred_top1 = names(sorted)[1]
    )
  })
  
  result
}

# Apply per outcome (this can take a few minutes)
ranks_full <- compute_rank_and_pred_what(all_test_preds)

cat("Total ranked predictions:", nrow(ranks_full), "\n")
write.csv(ranks_full,
          here("outputs", "reports_05_02", "ncss_test_ranks.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

plot_rank_with_pred <- function(rank_df, outcome_label) {
  rank_df <- rank_df %>%
    filter(!is.na(rank_of_truth)) %>%
    mutate(
      rank_category = case_when(
        rank_of_truth == 1 ~ "Rank 1\n(correct)",
        rank_of_truth == 2 ~ "Rank 2\n(close miss)",
        rank_of_truth == 3 ~ "Rank 3",
        rank_of_truth >= 4 & rank_of_truth <= 5 ~ "Rank 4-5",
        rank_of_truth >= 6 ~ "Rank 6+\n(off)"
      ),
      rank_category = factor(rank_category,
                             levels = c("Rank 1\n(correct)",
                                        "Rank 2\n(close miss)",
                                        "Rank 3",
                                        "Rank 4-5",
                                        "Rank 6+\n(off)"))
    )
  
  # Order classes by total count (most common at top)
  truth_order <- rank_df %>% count(truth) %>% arrange(n) %>% pull(truth)
  
  # For each true class, summarize what was predicted in misses
  rank_summary <- rank_df %>%
    count(truth, rank_category) %>%
    group_by(truth) %>%
    mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(truth = factor(truth, levels = truth_order))
  
  # Get top "predicted instead" per (truth × rank category) for misses
  predicted_instead <- rank_df %>%
    filter(rank_of_truth >= 2) %>%
    mutate(
      rank_category = case_when(
        rank_of_truth == 2 ~ "Rank 2\n(close miss)",
        rank_of_truth == 3 ~ "Rank 3",
        rank_of_truth >= 4 & rank_of_truth <= 5 ~ "Rank 4-5",
        rank_of_truth >= 6 ~ "Rank 6+\n(off)"
      ),
      rank_category = factor(rank_category,
                             levels = c("Rank 1\n(correct)",
                                        "Rank 2\n(close miss)",
                                        "Rank 3",
                                        "Rank 4-5",
                                        "Rank 6+\n(off)"))
    ) %>%
    count(truth, rank_category, pred_top1, sort = TRUE) %>%
    group_by(truth, rank_category) %>%
    slice_max(n, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    rename(top_pred_label = pred_top1)
  
  # Join the predicted-instead labels to the heatmap data
  rank_summary <- rank_summary %>%
    left_join(predicted_instead %>% select(truth, rank_category, top_pred_label),
              by = c("truth", "rank_category"))
  
  # Build cell labels: count + (predicted-instead) for misses
  rank_summary <- rank_summary %>%
    mutate(
      cell_label = case_when(
        n == 0 ~ "",
        rank_category == "Rank 1\n(correct)" ~ as.character(n),
        !is.na(top_pred_label) ~ paste0(n, "\n→ ", top_pred_label),
        TRUE ~ as.character(n)
      )
    )
  
  ggplot(rank_summary, aes(x = rank_category, y = truth, fill = prop)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white") +
    geom_text(aes(label = cell_label), size = 4, lineheight = 0.85) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#D55E00", mid = "#E69F00", high = "#009E73",
                         midpoint = 0.3,
                         limits = c(0, 1),
                         name = "Row prop") +
    labs(x = "Rank of truth in model's predictions",
         y = "True class",
         title = outcome_label) +
    thesis_theme +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5, size = 8),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
          panel.grid = element_blank(),
          legend.position = "right")
}

# One plot per outcome (no max_classes — show all)
rank_plots <- list()
outcomes_with_data <- ranks_full %>% distinct(outcome) %>% pull(outcome)

for (oc in outcomes_with_data) {
  rd <- ranks_full %>% filter(outcome == oc)
  if (nrow(rd) == 0) next
  rank_plots[[oc]] <- plot_rank_with_pred(rd, tax_labels[oc])
}

# Print one at a time first, then combine
for (oc in names(rank_plots)) {
  print(rank_plots[[oc]])
  ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, paste0("fig_ncss_rank_", oc, ".png")),
         rank_plots[[oc]],
         width = 11, height = 8, dpi = 300)
}
library(cowplot)

# Reorder by tax_order
ordered_plots_NCSS <- rank_plots[tax_order]
ordered_plots_NCSS <- ordered_plots_NCSS[!sapply(ordered_plots_NCSS, is.null)]

# Build a "with legend" version of the first plot just to extract the legend
first_outcome <- names(ordered_plots_NCSS)[1]
plot_with_legend <- plot_rank_with_pred(
  ranks_full %>% filter(outcome == first_outcome),
  tax_labels[first_outcome]
)

# Extract the legend grob
shared_legend <- get_legend(plot_with_legend)

# Remove legends from all panels
ordered_plots_NCSS_no_legend <- map(ordered_plots_NCSS, function(p) {
  p + scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#D55E00", mid = "#E69F00", high = "#009E73",
    midpoint = 0.3, limits = c(0, 1),
    guide = "none"
  )
})

# Build patchwork of panels (no legend on any)
panels <- wrap_plots(ordered_plots_NCSS_no_legend, ncol = 2) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Rank of true class in NCSS test predictions (best variant, all reps)",
    subtitle = "Cell color = row-proportion; cell text = count + (most common predicted instead for misses)",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))
  )

# Combine panels + legend in a horizontal layout
fig_ranks <- plot_grid(panels, shared_legend,
                       ncol = 2,
                       rel_widths = c(1, 0.08))

print(fig_ranks)
ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, "fig_ncss_rank_all.png"),
       fig_ranks, width = 16, height = 18, dpi = 300)
```


```{r}
#vip plot
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(patchwork)

fig_dir <- here("outputs", "figures_05_02")
dir.create(fig_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

thesis_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "top",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA))

tax_order  <- c("taxorder", "taxsuborder", "taxgrtgroup",
                "taxsubgrp_mod1", "taxpartsize", "taxonname_clean")
tax_labels <- c("Order", "Suborder", "Great Group",
                "Subgroup", "Particle Size", "Series")
names(tax_labels) <- tax_order

# ============================================================
# IDENTIFY BEST PLAIN VARIANT PER OUTCOME
# (no min5 override — uses whatever the bootstrap data shows is best)
# ============================================================
summary_clean <- read.csv(here("outputs", "reports_05_02",
                                "test_metrics_summary_clean.csv"))

best_plain_per_outcome <- summary_clean %>%
  filter(space == "plain") %>%
  group_by(outcome) %>%
  slice_max(kap_median, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(bundle_root = paste(outcome, engine, space, sep = "_"))

cat("Using bundles:\n")
print(best_plain_per_outcome %>% select(outcome, engine, space, bundle_root))

# ============================================================
# EXTRACT VIP FROM ALL 10 REPLICATE BUNDLES, AVERAGE
# ============================================================
extract_vip_from_bundle <- function(bundle_path) {
  b <- tryCatch(readRDS(bundle_path), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(b)) return(NULL)

  fit_obj <- workflows::extract_fit_engine(b$final_fit)

  imp <- tryCatch({
    if (inherits(fit_obj, "ranger")) {
      tibble(
        feature = names(fit_obj$variable.importance),
        importance = as.numeric(fit_obj$variable.importance)
      )
    } else if (inherits(fit_obj, "xgb.Booster")) {
      xgb_imp <- xgboost::xgb.importance(model = fit_obj)
      tibble(feature = xgb_imp$Feature, importance = xgb_imp$Gain)
    } else {
      return(NULL)
    }
  }, error = function(e) return(NULL))

  if (is.null(imp)) return(NULL)
  imp %>% mutate(importance = importance / sum(importance, na.rm = TRUE))
}

all_vip <- map_dfr(seq_len(nrow(best_plain_per_outcome)), function(i) {
  bundle_root <- best_plain_per_outcome$bundle_root[i]
  oc <- best_plain_per_outcome$outcome[i]

  cat("Processing", bundle_root, "...\n")

  rep_files <- list.files(here("outputs", "models_05_02"),
                          pattern = paste0("^", bundle_root,
                                            "_rep[0-9]+_bundle\\.rds$"),
                          full.names = TRUE)

  if (length(rep_files) == 0) {
    cat("  WARNING: no replicate bundles for", bundle_root, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }

  cat("  Found", length(rep_files), "replicate bundles\n")

  rep_vips <- map_dfr(rep_files, function(fp) {
    rep_num <- as.integer(str_extract(basename(fp), "(?<=_rep)[0-9]+"))
    v <- extract_vip_from_bundle(fp)
    if (is.null(v)) return(NULL)
    v %>% mutate(replicate = rep_num)
  })

  if (nrow(rep_vips) == 0) return(NULL)

  rep_vips %>%
    group_by(feature) %>%
    summarise(
      importance_median = median(importance, na.rm = TRUE),
      importance_lo     = quantile(importance, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
      importance_hi     = quantile(importance, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
      n_reps_present    = n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(outcome = oc, bundle_root = bundle_root)
})

write.csv(all_vip,
          here("outputs", "reports_05_02", "ncss_vip_median_across_reps.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nAll outcomes processed. Found data for:\n")
print(unique(all_vip$outcome))

# ============================================================
# PLOT WITH GRADIENT COLORS + SHARED X-AXIS LABEL PER COLUMN
# ============================================================
plot_vip <- function(vip_df, outcome_label, top_n = 15,
                     show_x_axis = TRUE) {
  d <- vip_df %>%
    arrange(desc(importance_median)) %>%
    slice_head(n = top_n) %>%
    mutate(feature = fct_reorder(feature, importance_median))

  p <- ggplot(d, aes(x = importance_median, y = feature,
                     fill = importance_median)) +
    geom_col() +
    geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = importance_lo, xmax = importance_hi),
                  width = 0.3, color = "grey30", linewidth = 0.4) +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "#FDAE61", high = "#08519C",
                        name = NULL, guide = "none") +
    labs(y = NULL, title = outcome_label) +
    thesis_theme +
    theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8))

  if (show_x_axis) {
    p <- p + labs(x = "Median normalized importance (IQR across 10 reps)")
  } else {
    p <- p + labs(x = NULL) +
      theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())
  }

  p
}

# Layout plan (2 columns):
# Row 1: Order      | Suborder
# Row 2: Great Grp  | Subgroup
# Row 3: Part Size  | Series   <-- bottom row gets x-axis label
vip_plots <- list()
for (i in seq_along(tax_order)) {
  oc <- tax_order[i]
  d <- all_vip %>% filter(outcome == oc)
  if (nrow(d) == 0) next

  # Only the last two plots (bottom row) show x-axis label
  is_bottom_row <- i %in% c(5, 6)
  vip_plots[[oc]] <- plot_vip(d, tax_labels[oc], top_n = 15,
                              show_x_axis = is_bottom_row)
}

# Combine — order matters for the row assignment
ordered_plots <- vip_plots[tax_order]
ordered_plots <- ordered_plots[!sapply(ordered_plots, is.null)]

fig_vip <- wrap_plots(ordered_plots, ncol = 2) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Top 15 predictors per taxonomic level (median across 10 NCSS train/test replicates)",
    subtitle = "Color gradient = median normalized importance; error bars = IQR across replicates",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
                  plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, color = "grey30"))
  )

print(fig_vip)
ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, "fig_ncss_vip_median.png"),
       fig_vip, width = 14, height = 16, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# NCSS CONFUSION MATRICES — one panel per outcome
# Uses each outcome's canonical bundle (best variant by NCSS kap_median)
# Single shared color-ramp legend
# Axis labels appear only on edge panels (left + bottom)
# ============================================================
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)
library(cowplot)
library(here)

fig_dir <- here("outputs", "figures_05_02")

thesis_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "top",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA))

tax_order  <- c("taxorder", "taxsuborder", "taxgrtgroup",
                "taxsubgrp_mod1", "taxpartsize", "taxonname_clean")
tax_labels <- c("Order", "Suborder", "Great Group",
                "Subgroup", "Particle Size", "Series")
names(tax_labels) <- tax_order

# ============================================================
# IDENTIFY BEST NCSS VARIANT PER OUTCOME (by kap_median)
# ============================================================
summary_clean <- read.csv(here("outputs", "reports_05_02",
                                "test_metrics_summary_clean.csv"))

best_ncss_per_outcome <- summary_clean %>%
  group_by(outcome) %>%
  slice_max(kap_median, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(bundle = paste(outcome, engine, space, sep = "_"))

# For series, prefer the min_n=5 deployment-realistic version if available
# (matches what the Perry figure shows). If the min_n=5 bundle isn't in
# summary_clean, fall back to whatever is best.
if ("taxonname_clean_min5_rf_plain" %in% list.files(here("outputs", "models_05_02"),
                                                     pattern = "_bundle\\.rds$") %>%
    str_remove("_bundle\\.rds$")) {
  # Use min5 if you want deployment parity with Perry; otherwise leave as-is
  # Uncomment if desired:
  # best_ncss_per_outcome <- best_ncss_per_outcome %>%
  #   mutate(bundle = if_else(outcome == "taxonname_clean",
  #                            paste0(bundle, "_min5_rf_plain"),
  #                            bundle))
}

cat("Best NCSS variant per outcome:\n")
print(best_ncss_per_outcome %>% select(outcome, engine, space, bundle, kap_median))

# ============================================================
# LOAD BUNDLES + BUILD CM DATA
# ============================================================
ncss_cm_plots <- list()

for (i in seq_len(nrow(best_ncss_per_outcome))) {
  oc <- best_ncss_per_outcome$outcome[i]
  bn <- best_ncss_per_outcome$bundle[i]

  bundle_path <- here("outputs", "models_05_02", paste0(bn, "_bundle.rds"))
  if (!file.exists(bundle_path)) {
    cat("Missing bundle for", oc, ":", basename(bundle_path), "\n")
    next
  }

  b <- readRDS(bundle_path)
  if (is.null(b$conf_mat)) {
    cat("No conf_mat in bundle for", oc, "\n")
    next
  }

  # Extract confusion matrix as long data
cm_df <- as.data.frame(b$conf_mat$table) %>%
    rename(truth_fct = Truth, pred_fct = Prediction, n = Freq) %>%
    group_by(truth_fct) %>%
    mutate(
      row_total = sum(n),
      prop = if (row_total[1] == 0) 0 else n / row_total[1]
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    select(-row_total)

  # Class ordering: most common truth at top of y-axis
  class_order <- cm_df %>%
    group_by(truth_fct) %>%
    summarise(total = sum(n), .groups = "drop") %>%
    arrange(total) %>%
    pull(truth_fct) %>% as.character()

  all_classes <- union(as.character(unique(cm_df$truth_fct)),
                       as.character(unique(cm_df$pred_fct)))
  x_order <- c(intersect(class_order, all_classes),
               setdiff(all_classes, class_order))

  cm_df <- cm_df %>%
    mutate(
      truth_fct = factor(as.character(truth_fct), levels = class_order),
      pred_fct  = factor(as.character(pred_fct),  levels = x_order)
    )

  # Determine which axis labels to show based on position in the 3x2 grid
  # Position in tax_order: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6
  # 2-column grid:
  #   row 1: outcomes 1, 2   -> bottom panels = NO (only show y-axis label)
  #   row 2: outcomes 3, 4   -> not edge
  #   row 3: outcomes 5, 6   -> bottom row (show x-axis label)
  # Y-axis label shown only on left column (odd positions)
  pos_in_order <- which(tax_order == oc)
  is_left_col   <- (pos_in_order %% 2) == 1   # 1, 3, 5
  is_bottom_row <- pos_in_order %in% c(5, 6)

  p <- ggplot(cm_df, aes(x = pred_fct, y = truth_fct, fill = prop)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
    geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(n > 0, n, "")), size = 6) +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "#2C7BB6",
                        limits = c(0, 1),
                        name = "Row prop") +
    labs(
      title = tax_labels[oc],
      x = if (is_bottom_row) "Predicted class" else NULL,
      y = if (is_left_col)   "True class"      else NULL
    ) +
    thesis_theme +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "right"
    )

  ncss_cm_plots[[oc]] <- p
}

cat("\nBuilt NCSS confusion-matrix plots for:",
    paste(names(ncss_cm_plots), collapse = ", "), "\n")

# ============================================================
# COMBINE INTO PATCHWORK + ADD SHARED LEGEND
# ============================================================
ordered_cm_plots <- ncss_cm_plots[tax_order]
ordered_cm_plots <- ordered_cm_plots[!sapply(ordered_cm_plots, is.null)]

# Extract the shared legend from the first plot (before stripping legends)
shared_legend_cm <- cowplot::get_legend(ordered_cm_plots[[1]])

# Strip legend from every panel
ordered_cm_plots_no_legend <- map(ordered_cm_plots, function(p) {
  p + scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "white", high = "#2C7BB6",
    limits = c(0, 1),
    guide = "none"
  )
})

# Build 2-column patchwork
cm_panels <- wrap_plots(ordered_cm_plots_no_legend, ncol = 2) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "NCSS confusion matrices across taxonomic levels",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))
  )

# Combine panels + shared legend horizontally
fig_ncss_cm <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  cm_panels, shared_legend_cm,
  ncol = 2,
  rel_widths = c(1, 0.08)
)

print(fig_ncss_cm)
ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, "fig_ncss_confusion_matrices.png"),
       fig_ncss_cm, width = 16, height = 15, dpi = 300)

cat("\nSaved: fig_ncss_confusion_matrices_05_15_2026.png\n")
```
